description: Poisoned hyperparameter: the PPO learning rate is inflated ~30x, which should collapse reward and inflate clip_frac. A healthy analysis points at ppo.lr in configs/base.yaml.
applied_diff:
--- a/configs/base.yaml
+++ b/configs/base.yaml
@@ -55,7 +55,7 @@
   seq_len: 16                # BPTT chunk; must divide rollout_steps
   epochs: 4
   minibatch_transitions: 256
-  lr: 3.0e-4
+  lr: 9.0e-3
   gamma: 0.99
   gae_lambda: 0.95
   clip: 0.2

proposals.rationale: Reward rolling mean dropped to -0.016 from a peak of 0.484 (raw logs) while the least-visited region holds only 0.521% of recorded positions — retrain/top-up suggested.
Reward slope is positive (0.0000/rollout) but position visitation is skewed (entropy 1.57 vs uniform 2.20) — rebalancing exploration may extend the gains (raw logs).
rssm/kl has sat at the free-nats floor (1.0) for the last 10 updates — the posterior is not using its budget (collapse indicator). Recommend lowering free_nats to 0.5 and observing kl/recon.
reward/rollout coefficient of variation is 4.7 and no logged scalar decomposes it — recommend adding `reward/per_region` so the variance source is attributable.
loss/total shows spike episodes (max jump 182.875) — instability argues for halving checkpoints.interval from 50000 (raw logs).
reward/rollout coefficient of variation is 4.7 and no logged scalar decomposes it — recommend adding `reward/per_episode_breakdown` so the variance source is attributable.
